name: St. John's
summary: St. John's is a colourful seaside city on the eastern tip of Canada, with rainbow houses tumbling down hills above a busy harbour. Whales, puffins and even icebergs cruise past its rocky shore each summer.
funFacts: Cape Spear, just outside the city, is the easternmost point of North America — stand on its rocks and nobody on the whole continent is further east than you.
On Signal Hill in 1901, inventor Guglielmo Marconi heard the very first wireless radio message ever sent across the Atlantic — three faint clicks tapped from England.
Every spring, gigantic 10,000-year-old icebergs drift down 'Iceberg Alley' from Greenland, sometimes floating right past St. John's harbour like floating glass mountains.
Witless Bay near St. John's is home to over 260,000 pairs of nesting Atlantic puffins, making it the biggest puffin city in North America.
Downtown's famous 'Jellybean Row' houses are painted purple, lime, pink and turquoise — locals started brightening up old wooden homes back in the 1970s.
thingsToDo: Climb Signal Hill to Cabot Tower for huge ocean views and the spot where the first transatlantic radio signal was heard.
Wander downtown to spot the candy-coloured Jellybean Row houses and count how many bright colours you can find.
Drive to Cape Spear Lighthouse and stand on the easternmost rocks in North America while watching for whales offshore.
Hop on a boat tour to spot Atlantic puffins, humpback whales and maybe a giant iceberg drifting past in summer.
Visit The Rooms museum, a giant building shaped like fishing sheds, to meet a real giant squid and explore Newfoundland history.
Wander tiny Quidi Vidi Village over the hill and watch fishing boats bob in one of the prettiest little harbours anywhere.

wildlife.name: Atlantic Puffin
wildlife.description: Newfoundland's small black-and-white provincial bird with a bright orange beak — nicknamed the 'sea parrot'.

wildlife.name: Newfoundland Dog
wildlife.description: Huge gentle black dog with webbed paws, famous for swimming and rescuing people from cold water.

wildlife.name: Humpback Whale
wildlife.description: Giant ocean animal that gathers here every summer in the world's biggest feeding population.

wildlife.name: Harp Seal
wildlife.description: Fluffy white pup that is born on floating sea ice and rides icebergs past the coast.

localWord.meaning: A super common Newfoundland expression meaning 'yes, friend!' — 'b'y' is short for 'boy' but means buddy or pal.

images.alt: Bright pink, yellow, blue and green row houses line a street in downtown St. John's.
The stone Cabot Tower stands tall on grassy Signal Hill above St. John's.
The white and red Cape Spear lighthouse sits at the easternmost point of North America.
Boats and the rocky entrance called the Narrows lead into St. John's harbour.
The Rooms museum building, shaped like giant fishing sheds, overlooks the city.
Tiny fishing sheds and boats line the rocky shores of Quidi Vidi village near St. John's.
An Atlantic puffin with a bright orange beak stands on a rocky cliff.
A big black Newfoundland dog with a thick fluffy coat sits outdoors.
A humpback whale's tail rises out of the ocean off the Newfoundland coast.
A fluffy white harp seal pup rests on snowy sea ice beside its mother.
